title: acetosa pratensis subsp. hibernica
intro: Acetosa pratensis subsp. hibernica, also known as Irish sorrel, is a subspecies of the sorrel plant, characterized by its reddish-purple leaves and edible stems. Traditionally, Irish sorrel has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation. Its leaves contain anthraquinones, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as aiding digestion and reducing joint pain. Irish sorrel can be prepared as a herbal tea, salve, or poultice, and it is also used in traditional remedies for its potential to support skin and wound healing.
